problems.title: What institutional redesigns can make long-term preferences politically load-bea
problems.summary: What institutional redesigns can make long-term preferences politically load-bearing without fatally weakening democratic accountability? This is an open tension because the conjecture implies a trade
problems.full_text: What institutional redesigns can make long-term preferences politically load-bearing without fatally weakening democratic accountability? This is an open tension because the conjecture implies a tradeoff: the same mechanisms that correct immediate harms also generate short-term bias. The problem is to identify designs—commitment devices, insulated agencies, multi-term budgets, automatic rules, new metrics—that preserve corrigibility, legitimacy, and adaptability while extending time horizons.

problems.title: What exact game-theoretic and institutional conditions make 'defensive specifica
problems.summary: What exact game-theoretic and institutional conditions make 'defensive specification' a Nash equilibrium, and when can coordinated subtraction become individually safe? The unresolved tension is that
problems.full_text: What exact game-theoretic and institutional conditions make 'defensive specification' a Nash equilibrium, and when can coordinated subtraction become individually safe? The unresolved tension is that the conjecture names an equilibrium but does not yet specify the players, payoffs, information structure, enforcement mechanisms, or credible commitment devices needed to model rule accretion and rule removal. This is worth pursuing because without a sharper model, proposed reforms like sunset clauses, bundled repeal, or blame-absorbing institutions remain underdetermined.

problems.title: How can the conjecture be operationalized and tested without collapsing into cir
problems.summary: How can the conjecture be operationalized and tested without collapsing into circularity? If survival is explained by self-correction and self-correction is inferred from survival, the theory risks ta
problems.full_text: How can the conjecture be operationalized and tested without collapsing into circularity? If survival is explained by self-correction and self-correction is inferred from survival, the theory risks tautology. A major open problem is to define observable indicators of 'goal corrigibility,' 'uncorrectable objectives,' and 'politics migrating outside constitutional channels' that allow comparative historical evaluation and possible refutation.
